fact_pattern: Wrongful death civil action following decedent's homicide; jury found defendant liable for compensatory and punitive damages.
source_quote: The jury awarded plaintiffs compensatory damages of $8.5 million and punitive damages totaling $25 million against defendant.

fact_pattern: Delivery truck driver lost control on wet roadway, crossed centerline, and struck plaintiff's vehicle head-on causing severe injuries.
source_quote: Plaintiff's economic damages, including past and future medical expenses and lost earning capacity, totaled $650,000.

fact_pattern: Public transit bus failed to yield at a controlled intersection and struck plaintiff's sedan on the driver side.
source_quote: The agency agreed to a $65,000 settlement covering plaintiff's medical bills and pain and suffering, the parties confirmed.

factors: Using a Wireless Telephone/Texting While Driving
injuries: L4-L5 disc herniation requiring epidural injections
fact_pattern: Rideshare driver checking app screen rear-ended plaintiff's stopped vehicle at a red light at moderate speed.
source_quote: Counsel confirmed the matter resolved for $285,000 prior to trial, including $95,000 in documented medical specials.

fact_pattern: Delivery truck merged unsafely on Interstate 5, sideswiping plaintiff's pickup and forcing it into the center divider.
source_quote: The Sacramento County jury returned a verdict of $920,000, allocating $320,000 to economic loss and $600,000 to general damages.

case_name: Okonkwo v. New York City Transit Auth.
injuries: Left tibial plateau fracture requiring ORIF
fact_pattern: MTA bus made a left turn across pedestrian crosswalk on signal, striking plaintiff and pinning her left leg.
source_quote: The Appellate Division upheld the jury's award of $750,000 for past and future medical care and $1.65 million for pain and suffering.

case_name: Weinstein v. Yellow Cab Co. of NYC
factors: Failure to Obey Traffic Control Device
fact_pattern: Taxi ran red light on Madison Avenue and 42nd Street, striking pedestrian crossing in marked crosswalk with walk signal.
source_quote: The parties agreed to a pre-trial settlement of $750,000, of which $220,000 represented documented medical expenses.

case_name: De La Cruz v. Amazon Logistics
injuries: C5-C6 disc herniation requiring anterior cervical discectomy and fusion
fact_pattern: Amazon delivery van rear-ended plaintiff's stopped vehicle in Bronx traffic at approximately 35 mph, causing severe whiplash trauma.
source_quote: The Bronx County jury returned a verdict totaling $1.45 million after finding Amazon's contractor 100% liable for the rear-end collision.

injuries: Aggravation of pre-existing degenerative disc disease
fact_pattern: Con Ed utility truck stopped abruptly without signaling on FDR Drive, causing plaintiff's vehicle to rear-end the truck.
source_quote: The utility resolved the action for $42,000, citing comparative fault and the plaintiff's pre-existing spinal condition.

fact_pattern: Intercity bus driver fatigued, drifted off I-87 at high speed, rolled the coach causing catastrophic passenger injuries.
source_quote: The jury awarded $2.1 million in past and future economic loss and $3.1 million for past and future pain and suffering.

fact_pattern: Grocery delivery truck made unsignaled right turn on Queens Boulevard, knocking down elderly pedestrian in crosswalk.
source_quote: Counsel confirmed the case resolved for $175,000 in mediation, including $55,000 attributable to medical specials.

fact_pattern: Walmart fleet truck rear-ended plaintiff's vehicle at signalized intersection in Dallas at approximately 40 mph.
source_quote: The Dallas County jury awarded plaintiff $240,000 for past medical care and $440,000 for past and future physical pain.

fact_pattern: Tractor-trailer driving too fast in icy conditions on I-20 lost control, crossed median, and struck family's pickup.
source_quote: The Harris County jury returned an $89.6 million verdict, including $16.8 million for past and future medical care for the surviving daughter.

fact_pattern: Commercial van made unsafe lane change on I-35 in Austin, sideswiping plaintiff's car and forcing it onto shoulder.
source_quote: The action settled for $95,000 in confidential mediation, with medical specials of approximately $32,000 documented.

injuries: Open right tibia/fibula fracture requiring intramedullary nailing
fact_pattern: Off-duty employee driving company vehicle while intoxicated crossed centerline on rural highway, striking plaintiff head-on.
source_quote: The jury awarded $800,000 in economic damages, $1.7 million in non-economic damages, and $700,000 in exemplary damages.

case_name: Lopez v. Metropolitan Transit Auth. of Harris County
defendant: Metropolitan Transit Authority of Harris County
injuries: Right femoral neck fracture requiring total hip arthroplasty
fact_pattern: METRO bus pulled away from stop while plaintiff was still boarding, throwing her to the pavement and fracturing her hip.
source_quote: The transit agency agreed to pay $250,000, the statutory cap on tort liability for governmental units under Texas law.

factors: Failure to Obey Traffic Control Device
injuries: T12 burst fracture with incomplete spinal cord injury
fact_pattern: Beer distributor truck ran stop sign in west Houston industrial area, T-boning plaintiff's sedan at high rate of speed.
source_quote: The Harris County jury returned a $4.8 million verdict including $1.9 million for past and future medical and life-care expenses.

injuries: Right ankle trimalleolar fracture requiring ORIF with hardware
fact_pattern: Hertz rental customer attempted unsafe pass on Pacific Coast Highway and clipped plaintiff's vehicle, forcing it off shoulder.
source_quote: The matter resolved for $215,000 prior to depositions, with documented orthopedic and surgical specials of $78,000.

case_name: Alvarez v. Clovis Unified School District
injuries: Closed head injury with mild traumatic brain injury
fact_pattern: School district van failed to yield to crossing guard signal, striking minor pedestrian leaving elementary school campus.
source_quote: The Fresno County jury awarded the minor $175,000 in past and future medical expenses and $350,000 in general damages.

case_name: O'Brien v. City of New York (Dep't of Sanitation)
factors: Fleeing the Scene of a Collision
injuries: Right shoulder rotator cuff tear requiring arthroscopic repair
fact_pattern: Sanitation truck stopped abruptly in travel lane, struck plaintiff's vehicle, and operator briefly left scene before returning.
source_quote: The jury found the City 60% liable and awarded plaintiff $38,000 after apportionment for comparative negligence.
